session.feature_description: Some AI assistant to make work easier.

release_spec.problem: The feature description is too vague to precisely identify the problem being solved.
target_users: Unclear – description does not specify a domain or role.
core_use_cases: Unknown – user must provide more context (domain, workflow, target users).
non_functional_requirements: User must refine the description before any real readiness assessment.
success_metrics: Description is updated with: domain, user roles, primary workflow, and expected outcomes.

risks.title: Misaligned risk analysis due to vague scope
risks.description: Without a clear domain or workflow, any suggested risks are speculative.
mitigation_ideas: Prompt the user for more detail before generating a full risk register.
Mark all generated risks as tentative.
Encourage selecting a domain template (e.g., support, legal, finance).

checklist.item: Specify domain (e.g., legal, customer support, finance) and data sensitivity.
Identify primary user roles and core tasks the assistant will support.
Clarify whether the assistant interacts with end users or internal staff only.

levels.description: No meaningful AI evaluation can be defined yet due to missing context.
notes: Ask the user to refine the feature description before running meaningful AI evaluation.
Re-run readiness check after requirements have been completed — this output is illustrative only.

test_cases.name: Vague input warning
test_cases.abstract_input: Provide a generic description such as 'AI to help us at work'.
test_cases.expected_behavior: System issues a clear warning and checklist items instead of pretending to do a full readiness assessment.

guardrails.name: Low-confidence banner
guardrails.description: Show a prominent warning in the UI when confidence is low.
guardrails.implementation_idea: If meta.confidence == 'Low', display a yellow banner with a message: 'Description is too vague – please refine before acting on this snapshot.'

release_notes.title: Readiness Snapshot – Vague Description Only
release_notes.summary: LaunchGuard detected that the feature description is too vague for a meaningful readiness assessment.
whats_new: Low-confidence detection and warnings for incomplete descriptions.
why_it_matters: Prevents teams from developing false confidence in incomplete outputs.
Encourages better problem definition before implementation.
known_limitations: No domain-specific risks or tests are generated at this stage.

landing_copy.hero_title: We need more detail to help you.
landing_copy.hero_subtitle: Describe who this is for, what they do, and what success looks like.
landing_copy.tagline: Good governance starts with a clear problem definition.
key_benefits: Pushes teams to clarify their ideas instead of skipping straight to implementation.
Prevents misleading readiness snapshots when context is missing.

feature_sections.title: Why clarity matters
feature_sections.body: Without knowing the domain, user roles, and data sensitivity, any readiness assessment is guesswork.

trust_and_safety.headline: We'd rather say 'we don't know' than mislead you.
bullets: Encourages better problem framing before shipping AI features.

slides.title: Why vague AI ideas are dangerous
slides.objective: Show the audience why ill-defined AI projects generate real risks — not just inconvenience.
key_points: Vague feature descriptions produce speculative risk registers and misleading test plans.
Teams that ship without clarity create systems that are hard to govern and impossible to audit.
LaunchGuard stops and asks for more detail rather than faking precision.
notes_for_speaker: Start with a relatable failure: a team that shipped an AI feature described as “AI to help users” — with no defined scope, users, or success criteria.
The point is not to embarrass — it’s to show that vagueness is a governance failure, not just a planning one.
Set up the next slide by asking: what does a responsible system do when it detects vagueness?

slides.title: LaunchGuard’s low-confidence guardrail
slides.objective: Demonstrate that responsible AI tooling surfaces uncertainty rather than hiding it.
key_points: Navigator detects when confidence is Low and sets needs_more_detail = true.
The system generates a diagnostic checklist of missing fields instead of a false-precision output.
Users see exactly what information is needed before a real readiness assessment can run.
notes_for_speaker: Show the vague scenario output in LaunchGuard: the low-confidence banner, the sparse risk register, the diagnostic checklist.
Emphasise the contrast: a generic AI tool would generate a polished but useless output. LaunchGuard tells the truth.
This slide proves that the system is calibrated, not just confident.

slides.title: What good looks like – the path forward
slides.objective: Close the loop by showing what a refined description unlocks and what the next step is.
key_points: Add domain, user roles, specific workflow, and risk sensitivity — and rerun the pipeline.
The legal and high-risk demos show what a complete LaunchGuard analysis delivers.
Clarity is not just good practice — it’s the precondition for safe AI deployment.
notes_for_speaker: Pivot to one of the polished demo scenarios to show the contrast.
The message: vagueness is fixable — LaunchGuard shows you exactly what to fix.
Close with: “If you can’t describe your feature clearly enough for LaunchGuard, you’re not ready to build it.”
